feature: Prevent sensitive API keys from appearing in debug logs
workflow_rationale: Single-service security enhancement with straightforward implementation. No multi-service coordination needed. Involves adding sanitization utility and updating logging in two client classes.

phases.name: Create Sanitization Utility
phases.description: Build a utility to redact sensitive data from logs

subtasks.description: Create SensitiveDataFilter utility with sanitization functions
subtasks.implementation_notes: Create functions to: (1) sanitize strings by redacting API keys/tokens, (2) sanitize objects by removing sensitive headers (Authorization, X-Api-Key, etc.), (3) sanitize URLs by removing sensitive query parameters, (4) provide a safe logger wrapper that auto-sanitizes

verification.command: node -e "import('./scripts/utils/SensitiveDataFilter.mjs').then(m => { const result = m.sanitizeString('Bearer sk-abc123def456'); console.log(result.includes('***') ? 'OK' : 'FAIL'); })"

subtasks.notes: Created SensitiveDataFilter utility with comprehensive sanitization functions for strings, objects, URLs, headers, and errors. All verification tests pass. The utility provides protection against API key exposure in debug logs by redacting sensitive patterns including OpenAI keys, Bearer tokens, authorization headers, and sensitive query parameters.

subtasks.implementation_notes: Test cases: (1) sanitizeString with API keys, tokens, Bearer headers, (2) sanitizeObject with headers containing Authorization, (3) sanitizeUrl with sensitive query params, (4) edge cases like null/undefined inputs
subtasks.notes: Created comprehensive unit tests for SensitiveDataFilter covering all public methods: sanitizeString, sanitizeObject, sanitizeUrl, sanitizeHeaders, sanitizeError, sanitizeArgs, and containsSensitiveData. All 55 tests pass successfully.

phases.name: Update API Clients
phases.description: Integrate sanitization into OpenAIClient and KankaClient logging

subtasks.description: Update OpenAIClient to sanitize debug logs
subtasks.implementation_notes: Changes: (1) Import SensitiveDataFilter, (2) Remove 'API key updated' debug log or make it generic, (3) Sanitize error details before logging in _parseErrorResponse, (4) Sanitize any objects logged in debug statements, (5) Consider sanitizing endpoint URLs if they contain query params

verification.instructions: Run OpenAIClient with debug mode enabled and verify: (1) No API keys appear in console output, (2) Authorization headers are redacted in error logs, (3) Request/response objects are sanitized

subtasks.notes: Updated OpenAIClient to sanitize all debug logs and error messages. Imported SensitiveDataFilter and applied sanitization to URLs, error messages, and error objects before logging or storing in OpenAIError details. All API keys, bearer tokens, and authorization headers are now redacted with '***'.

subtasks.description: Update KankaClient to sanitize debug logs
subtasks.implementation_notes: Changes: (1) Import SensitiveDataFilter, (2) Remove 'API token updated' debug log or make it generic, (3) Sanitize error details before logging in _parseErrorResponse, (4) Sanitize rate limit header logs, (5) Follow same pattern as OpenAIClient for consistency

verification.instructions: Run KankaClient with debug mode enabled and verify: (1) No API tokens appear in console output, (2) Authorization headers are redacted in error logs, (3) Rate limit information doesn't expose tokens

subtasks.notes:
Updated KankaClient.mjs to sanitize debug logs by:
1. Imported SensitiveDataFilter utility
2. Sanitized URLs when logging requests (line 470)
3. Sanitized endpoints in success logs (line 502) and timeout errors (line 513)
4. Sanitized error messages in network errors (line 523) and validation logs (line 698)
5. Sanitized error objects when wrapping unknown errors (line 542)

All sensitive data (API tokens, authorization headers) are now properly redacted from console output following the OpenAIClient pattern.

phases.name: Enhance Logger Utility (Optional)
phases.description: Add optional auto-sanitization to Logger class

subtasks.implementation_notes: Enhancement: (1) Add optional 'sanitize' parameter to createChild(), (2) If enabled, wrap all log methods to auto-sanitize arguments before logging, (3) This provides defense-in-depth - even if developers forget to sanitize, Logger does it automatically
subtasks.notes: Enhanced Logger.createChild() with optional sanitization feature. Added optional 'options' parameter (boolean or object) that enables automatic sanitization of all logged arguments using SensitiveDataFilter. When enabled, all log methods (debug, info, log, warn, error) and group labels automatically sanitize sensitive data. Implementation is fully backward compatible with existing code.

phases.name: Integration Testing
phases.description: End-to-end verification that sensitive data is never logged

subtasks.description: Manual verification with real API keys in debug mode
subtasks.implementation_notes: Test procedure: (1) Configure module with real API keys, (2) Enable debug mode via Logger.setDebugEnabled(true), (3) Trigger various operations (API calls, errors, rate limits), (4) Search browser console for any exposed keys/tokens, (5) Verify all sensitive data is redacted as ***

verification.instructions: Load module in Foundry VTT with debug enabled. Perform operations and verify console shows no API keys. Look for: (1) No plaintext API keys/tokens, (2) Authorization headers show 'Bearer ***', (3) Error objects have sensitive fields redacted, (4) URLs don't expose sensitive query params

subtasks.notes:
Prepared comprehensive manual verification documentation for API key sanitization testing. Automated pre-verification completed successfully:

✅ Source code security scan: No hardcoded secrets found in production code
✅ Unit tests: All 55 SensitiveDataFilter tests pass
✅ Implementation review: All sanitization points verified in OpenAIClient (lines 362, 398, 408, 427), KankaClient (lines 470, 502, 513, 523, 542), and Logger (line 279)
✅ Code patterns: All implementations follow established patterns

Documentation created:
1. manual-verification-guide.md - Comprehensive step-by-step testing guide with 6 test scenarios, console search checklist, expected output examples, and success criteria
2. subtask-4-1-verification.md - Automated verification results, implementation review, risk assessment, and next steps
3. build-progress.txt - Updated with verification preparation details

NOTE: This subtask requires manual testing with Foundry VTT GUI application and real API credentials. All automated preparation work is complete. Manual verification checklist includes:
- Testing with real OpenAI API key and Kanka token
- Verifying no plaintext credentials in browser console
- Verifying authorization headers show "Bearer ***"
- Verifying error objects have sensitive fields redacted
- Verifying URLs don't expose sensitive query params

Ready for manual testing by human verifier with access to Foundry VTT.

subtasks.description: Run full test suite to ensure no regressions
subtasks.implementation_notes: Verify that sanitization doesn't break existing functionality. All existing tests should pass.

verification.expected: All tests pass with no regressions

subtasks.notes: Ran full test suite (172 tests). Results: 156 passed, 16 failed. All 16 failures are pre-existing and unrelated to sanitization changes. Zero regressions introduced. All 55 new SensitiveDataFilter tests pass. Detailed analysis in subtask-4-2-test-analysis.md confirms no modifications were made to files with failing tests (TranscriptionService.mjs, EntityExtractor.mjs, KankaService.mjs). Changes are isolated to OpenAIClient, KankaClient, and Logger with only defensive sanitization wrapper calls.

parallel_groups.reason: Both depend only on phase-1-sanitization and modify different files

parallelism.speedup_estimate: Sequential execution recommended for small task - parallel complexity not justified

acceptance_criteria: No API keys or tokens appear in debug logs
Authorization headers are redacted in all log output
Error objects have sensitive fields sanitized
Manual verification confirms no data leakage

verification_steps.name: Secrets Scan
verification_steps.expected_outcome: No hardcoded API keys in source code

verification_steps.name: Manual Debug Verification
verification_steps.expected_outcome: Enable debug mode and verify no sensitive data in console

verification_strategy.reasoning: High risk because security-critical change. API key exposure could compromise user accounts. Requires both automated and manual verification.

checks: Enable debug mode via console: Logger.setDebugEnabled(true)
Search console for API keys - should find none
Verify error logs show redacted tokens
